MOTION Notice Of Motion & Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Feb-05-2003 Continued To Mar-06-03 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONERS' ROGER W WALDENSTROM, and MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award:

Appearances:
BEN SUTER, Esq. and CARA L. MEREDITH, Esq. of Keesal, Young & Logan, (415) 398-6000, on behalf of petitioners and cross-respondents ROGER W. WALDENSTROM, and MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH and moving party.
MELINDA JANE STEUER, Esq. of Law Offices of Howard M. Hoffman, (916) 368-6000, on behalf of respondent and cross-petitioner HARVEY T. BIDDLE and opposing party.

* The Court gave its tentative ruling as follows: The Court remands this matter to the arbitration panel to consider evidence offered in respondent's brief regarding fraudulent recommendations by Merrill Lynch, and to consider request for discovery of documents and to make findings in respect to whether any of the stock Mr. Biddle lost money were stocks that Mr. Blodget recommended fraudulently. Having heard oral arguments, the Court continued this matter to March 6, 2003 at 3:30 p.m. in Dept. 503. Mr. Hoffman to appear for this hearing. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: V. MU; REPORTER: KAREN FARNSWORTH, CSR #5162 (RS) (503)

Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award; Request For Judicial Notice

MINI MINUTES: CROSS-PETITIONER HARVEY T BIDDLE's Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award:

Appearances:
MELINDA JANE STEUER, Esq. of Law Offices of Howard M. Hoffman, (916) 368-6000, on behalf of respondent and cross-petitioner HARVEY T. BIDDLE and moving party.
BEN SUTER, Esq. and CARA L. MEREDITH, Esq. of Keesal, Young & Logan, (415) 398-6000, on behalf of petitioners and cross-respondents ROGER W. WALDENSTROM, and MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH and opposing party

* The Court gave its tentative ruling as follows: The Court remands this matter to the arbitration panel to consider evidence offered in respondent's brief regarding fraudulent recommendations by Merrill Lynch, and to consider request for discovery of documents and to make findings in respect to whether any of the stock Mr. Biddle lost money were stocks that Mr. Blodget recommended fraudulently. Having heard oral arguments, the Court continued this matter to March 6, 2003 at 3:30 p.m. in Dept. 503. Mr. Hoffman to appear for this hearing. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: V. MU; REPORTER: KAREN FARNSWORTH, CSR #5162 (RS) (503)
